title: Advanced Implementation Judgment
prompt: A plugin proposal uses a service container, five interfaces, React on the frontend, and a custom router for one settings page and one shortcode. Review the engineering judgment and propose a simpler architecture.
hints: The right answer is not anti-abstraction. It is pro-fit.

model_answer.summary: The proposed architecture is overbuilt for one settings page and one shortcode; keep the useful seams and remove ceremony that does not buy clarity.
model_answer.work: PHP 8.1 features such as typed properties, readonly value objects, and constructor promotion are useful, but a service container and five interface layers are not justified unless the plugin has real interchangeable implementations. Use WordPress API entry points directly: one Settings class for the admin page, one Shortcode class registered through add_shortcode, and a small REST controller only if JavaScript needs async data. Use JavaScript only for interactions that improve the screen; a normal Settings API form is simpler and more maintainable than a React app for static fields. Keep one interface only where tests or a replaceable upstream client truly need it.
checks: Review whether each interface has more than one expected implementation.
Verify the shortcode works without the custom router.
Test any REST endpoint with capability and nonce checks.
Run PHPCS and php -l to confirm the simpler PHP 8.1 structure is clean.
Smoke test the settings page with JavaScript disabled if the form can work without it.
model_answer.why_this_is_good: It does not reject modern PHP; it insists that abstractions prove their value in the actual plugin shape.
common_misses: Treating every interface as automatically good architecture.
Using a custom router where WordPress routing already exists.
Shipping React for a form that does not need client state.
Skipping tests after simplifying the structure.
model_answer.revision_prompt: Revise by naming which abstractions stay, which go, how WordPress APIs replace custom routing, and how the simpler design is verified.

summary.description: One or two sentences explaining the answer.

work.description: The main implementation, review, or design answer.

checks.description: Verification steps, review checks, or acceptance criteria.
